content.problemStatement:
The integers 000 to M−1M - 1M−1 have been arranged in a circular fashion. That is, 0,1,2,…,M−10, 1, 2, \ldots, M - 10,1,2,…,M−1, are in that order and
also, 000 and M−1M - 1M−1 are next to each other. The distance between any two adjacent numbers on this circle is 1. You are given NNN intervals on this, such that no two intervals touch or intersect with each other. The i-th interval will be of the form [Li,RiL_i, R_iLi​,Ri​]. This means that the i-th interval contains all the integers between LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​, both end points inclusive. You are supposed to mark exactly one number inside each interval, in such a way that the minimum distance between any two marked numbers is maximized.

More formally, we have 0≤L1≤R1<L2≤R2<L3…<LN≤RN≤M−10 \leq L_1 \leq R_1 < L_2 \leq R_2 < L_3 \ldots < L_N \leq R_N \leq M - 10≤L1​≤R1​<L2​≤R2​<L3​…<LN​≤RN​≤M−1. You are supposed to mark exactly NNN numbers: A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​, such that Li≤Ai≤RiL_i \leq A_i \leq R_iLi​≤Ai​≤Ri​ for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N. And you want to do it in such a manner mini≠jmin_{i \neq j}mini=j​ (shortest distance between AiA_iAi​ and AjA_jAj​ ), is maximized.

Input:
First line of the input contains a pair of integers MMM and NNN.
The i-th of the next NNN lines contains two numbers LiL_iLi​ and RiR_iRi​ which denote the end points of the i-th interval.
Output:
A single integer denoting the maximized minimum distance between any two marked numbers.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
We can choose A1=0,A2=3,A3=6A_1 = 0, A_2 = 3, A_3 = 6A1​=0,A2​=3,A3​=6. The distance between every adjacent marked pair of numbers is 3, and hence that is the minimum. You can check that you cannot do any better, and hence 3 is the answer.

solution.approach: The code idea is to use a binary search technique to find the minimum number of months required to gather at least **W** meters of wood. Start with two variables representing the lower and upper bounds for the months. For each midpoint value of months, calculate the total height of all trees after that many months, only considering those that meet the minimum height requirement. Adjust the search bounds based on whether the collected wood meets the order requirement. Continue until the optimal number of months is found, then return that value.
